tools.name: shahar_load_data
tools.description: Loads, merges, and cleans raw geochemistry, mineralogy, and coordinate datasets from Google Cloud Storage (GCS) or local templates into a Shahar session. Triggers the data-ingestion pipeline.

session_id.description: Unique session UUID to track the data pipeline context.

primary_path.description: Cloud or local path to primary assays spreadsheet (contains major and trace element geochemical data).

minory_path.description: Optional path to minor trace elements spreadsheet (TOC, TCM, Hg, Sb, As, Tl pathfinder suites).

coordinates_path.description: Optional path to spatial coordinates spreadsheet (X, Y, Z depth profiles).

use_clr.description: Whether to apply Centered Log-Ratio (CLR) transformation to handle compositional geochemistry constraints.

tools.geological_context: Initializes the mineral exploration dataset. Essential first step to prepare the raw assay spreadsheets for high-dimensional clustering.

tools.name: shahar_run_clustering
tools.description: Trains a traditional k-Means or Self-Organizing Map (SOM) clustering pipeline on the loaded dataset to segment ore into distinct geochemical and mineralogical zones.

n_clusters.description: Number of clusters. If using SOM, represents the grid clusters size.

use_pca.description: Whether to run PCA dimensionality reduction before clustering to isolate key geological vectors.

n_components.description: Number of principal components to keep (null for auto).

tools.geological_context: Calibrates geological zones. Useful for separating unaltered limestone, silicified jasperoids, argillic shales, and pyritic feeder conduits based on elemental associations.

tools.name: shahar_apply_som
tools.description: Applies the calibrated PCA-SOM or k-Means clustering model to unseen, raw production (apply.xlsx) samples in the active session.

session_id.description: Active session ID containing the trained model.

data_mode.description: Scope of the production data columns.

tools.geological_context: Partitions unseen exploration drill holes or production stockpiles into predicted geo-metallurgical domains.

tools.name: kokhav_load_data
tools.description: Loads and merges geochemical assays, QEMSCAN mineral percentages, and recovery outcomes inside a Kokhav session for machine learning modeling.

minory_path.description: Path to minor/pathfinder elements spreadsheet (Au, As, Sb, Hg, Tl, TOC, TCM).

qemscan_path.description: Path to mineralogical percentages sheet (QEMSCAN: pyrite, quartz, carbonates, clays).

recovery_path.description: Path to recovery and grinding indices sheet (calibration outcomes).

tools.geological_context: Registers historical calibration core assays and QEMSCAN mineralogies to train regression models for metal recovery and comminution hardness.

tools.name: kokhav_predict
tools.description: Applies trained metallurgical machine learning models to predict comminution grinding hardness (BWI) or flotation recovery for unseen samples.

model_key.description: Identifier of the trained ML model, e.g. 'recovery_random_forest' or 'grinding_xgboost'.

target_dataset.description: Target dataset column block inside the session (e.g. 'merged' or 'engineered').

handle_missing.description: Handling strategy for missing elements (impute uses Clarke background values).

tools.geological_context: Provides critical commercial insights by predicting comminution energy costs and metal recoveries prior to actual physical milling.

tools.name: kokhav_shap_plots
tools.description: Computes SHAP feature importance values and returns a beeswarm visualization explaining why certain mineral assemblages drive recovery.

top_n.description: Number of top features to report in the importance report.

tools.geological_context: Validates machine learning logic by exposing how mineral assemblages (e.g. illite clay penalties, pyrite locking) mathematically drive flotation recovery.

tools.name: keter_classify_tectonic
tools.description: Classifies the geotectonic basin environment (e.g. Foreland Basin, Passive Margin) based on raw trace element geochemistry and coordinates.

fingerprint.description: Key-value mapping of element concentrations (ppm) or Enrichment Factors (EF).

strict_lithology.description: If true, enforces strict Layer -1 lithological zero-masking. If false, opens Hybrid Mode.

tools.geological_context: Determines the fundamental geodynamic setting. Essential for setting the Bayesian background Clarkes and establishing tectonic control priors.

tools.name: keter_classify_ore
tools.description: Evaluates geochemical fingerprint against Bayesian ore subtype cartridges to classify incoming ore into mineralized subcategories (high_carbon, low_carbon, argillite).

fingerprint.description: Key-value mapping of elements (Au, As, Sb, Hg, Tl, TOC, TCM, carbonates).

tools.geological_context: Determines the precise ore alteration profile. The resulting class (e.g. double refractory high-carbon) directly overrides roasting optimization constraints.

tools.name: teomim_activate_nodes
tools.description: The Geo-Thermo Handshake endpoint. Automatically overrides roaster constraints and reward weights based on Keter's geological ore classification.
tools.geological_context: Links geology directly to metallurgical control. High-carbon ore injects penalties for low temperature to burn off carbon, and argillite ore lowers sintering thresholds by 20°C.

tools.name: teomim_optimize_thermo
tools.description: Runs the Bayesian Kiln Optimizer to recommend the optimal roaster action vectors (feed rates, particle size, burner tilt, excess air) that maximize gold recovery while satisfying all physical safety bounds.

constraints.description: List of physical or environmental safety constraints.

baseline_readings.description: Current physical readings of the roaster.

n_iterations.description: Number of optimization calls (50 is recommended for Bayesian).

use_adaptive_reward.description: Enables historical memory penalties to prevent physical oscillating loops.

tools.geological_context: Computes the final operational recommendations for the roaster plant, finding the sweet spot between full oxidation and thermal sintering.

tools.name: teomim_interaction_heatmap
tools.description: Computes a 2D interaction grid sweeping two kiln parameters (e.g. feed_rate_tph, excess_air_pct, particle_p80_um, tertiary_air_temp_c) against an output variable (e.g. gold_recovery_pct, blockage_risk, sintering_risk, as2o3_emissions_mg_nm3) for precise plotting.

output_variable.description: Metallurgical output or risk indicator to map.

n_a.description: Number of grid divisions for variable A.

n_b.description: Number of grid divisions for variable B.

tools.geological_context: Maps non-linear physical interactions (e.g., how particle size limits gas diffusion or causes sintering) to assist in roaster operating envelope definition.

tools.name: teomim_pareto_frontier
tools.description: Discovers the Pareto frontier trade-offs between two competing objectives (e.g., maximizing gold_recovery_pct vs minimizing as2o3_emissions_mg_nm3, or minimizing blockage_risk vs maximizing heat_efficiency_pct). Useful for industrial multiobjective trade-offs.

n_samples.description: Number of Monte Carlo Latin Hypercube samples.

tools.geological_context: Resolves multi-objective industrial dilemmas by locating non-dominated, Pareto-optimal operational envelopes that balance commercial efficiency and strict environmental compliance.

tools.name: search_historical_logs
tools.description: Searches the historical kiln incident reports and operational logs for past failures, sintering collapses, or environmental emissions exceedances linked to specific ore/mineral subtypes at Nevada Carlin sites.

query.description: Keywords or ore types to search for (e.g., 'high carbon', 'argillite', 'sintering', 'arsenic').

tools.geological_context: Enables proactive RAG-based operational safety checks. Ensures the agent reviews and learns from past physical malfunctions before executing new thermodynamic optimizations.

tools.name: run_multi_agent_negotiation
tools.description: Triggers the cooperative/conflicting multi-agent ore negotiation between the Geologist Agent (seeking highest Au grades) and the Metallurgist Agent (seeking safe roaster bounds). Computes the optimal blend, resolves trade-offs, and outputs a step-by-step bargaining transcript.

target_recovery.description: Desired minimum target gold recovery percentage.

tools.geological_context: Resolves agent-to-agent criteria. Geologist and Metallurgist bargain dynamically to balance maximum grade feed against environmental and sintering bounds.
